content.problemStatement:
As we all know, Sudhanva is fond of reading books and taking photographs. His dad (after gifting him an iPad last birthday) gifted him a custom-made bookshelf this birthday. This shelf is made in such a way that books can be inserted and removed only from the top. Books are placed one over the other in that shelf. Now to remember each book, Sudhanva develops an algorithm. He first writes a number in his iPad, then takes a photo of that number in his DSLR and then pastes that photograph on the cover of each book. Initially the bookshelf is empty.

You have given QQQ queries of either of the two types

•	Type 1. (1,N)(1, N)(1,N) In this type of query Sudhanva places a book with photo of number N on the top. You don't have to print anything in this query

•	Type 2.  (−1)(-1)(−1) In this Query Sudhanva takes out the topmost book, you have to print the number on the book he just took out. If the shelf is empty and Sudhanva is trying to take out a book, print "kuchbhi?" Without quotes

Input:
First line will contain QQQ, number of queries
Following QQQ lines will contain queries of one of the types mentioned
Type 1:    111 NNN
This query specifies that Sudhanva places a book with number NNN on it, at the top of the shelf.
Type 2:    −1-1−1
This query specifies that Sudhanva takes out the topmost books from the shelf.
Output:For each query of Type 2, print the number NNN on the book he just took out, if the shelf was empty and it was not possible to take out a book, then print "kuchbhi?" (without quotes)  Each output should be printed on a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer Q, the number of queries. The next Q lines each contain a query. A query is either of the form "1 N" (push a book with number N onto the top of the shelf) or "-1" (pop the topmost book).
content.outputFormat: For each query of type "-1", output on a separate line the number written on the removed book. If the shelf is empty when a pop is requested, output the string "kuchbhi?" (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Push 2, push 45, pop -> 45, pop -> 2, pop on empty -> kuchbhi?.

solution.approach: Maintain a stack that stores the numbers on the books. For a push query (type 1) push N onto the stack. For a pop query (type -1) check if the stack is empty: if not, pop the top element and print it; otherwise print "kuchbhi?". The algorithm processes each query in O(1) time, giving overall O(Q) time and O(Q) auxiliary space in the worst case (when all queries are pushes).
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q) – each query is handled in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(Q) – at most Q numbers can be stored in the stack.
